sections.title: Android Architecture
sections.desc: Deep understanding of the Android SDK and Lifecycle, using MVVM to to build clean, scalable apps.

sections.title: Languages & Core
sections.desc: Focused on Kotlin first development. Using Coroutines & Flows to build high performance, state driven apps with modern interfaces and smooth UX.

sections.title: Dependency Injection
sections.desc: Using Koin DI to keep the codebase clean and organized, making features easier to build, test, and update.

sections.title: CI/CD
sections.desc: Designing and building automated testing, build and release pilelines and workflows.

sections.title: FuelFind
sections.desc: Data-Driven Refueling: Real-Time Fuel Price Price Analytics, Route Optimization & Driving Companion.

details.about: FuelFind is in development. Screenshots are not representational of a production build. FuelFind is like any other Fuel Price app, using realtime API data. But better. Free, without ads, highly performant, with widgets. This is an app for the casual driver, the car freaks and the nerds. At your fingertips fuel prices for unleaded, premium, diesel, and electric in app or on your homescreen (widgets), for any station, across the country. With advanced features like: • Push Notifications: For realtime price alerts. • Historical Pricing Data • Route Planning: Driving long distance? Need to refuel? Be efficient and cost effective with FuelFind.

sections.title: Lifetime
sections.desc: An event tracker and memory journal

details.about: Lifetime is WIP and the project scope is subject to change. Screenshots are not representational of a production build. Lifetime is a way of tracking upcoming events like birthdays & holidays and remembering past events, like meeting your partner or giving up a habit.

Live Departures - Map - Stations - Travel Alerts - Widgets - WearOS Companion A Manchester Metrolink Travel App MetroBee was my vision for catching a tram in Manchester, before the Bee Network became a thing... Unfortunately Transport for Greater Manchester decided to deprecate their open data API, with no replacement I was forced to abandon the project.

sections.title: Ember
sections.desc: A dating companion app that serves pickup lines and puns for any situation.

details.about: Moving away from Java, Ember is my first full Kotlin app.

sections.title: Attack Master
sections.desc: A reward aggregator and distrubution service for CoinMaster by MoonActive.

details.about:
Attack Master is my most successful project to date and rides on the success of Coin Master by Moon Active.

Attack Master collates and provides in game reward links from across social media and email newsletters. Users can opt in to receive notifications for the latest rewards.

Written natively for Android in Java with Android Studio.

Attack Master accrued over 3 million downloads, over 100,000 DAU (daily active users) and 400,000 WAU (weekly active users) with ads being served from Admob and Unity Ads for mediation performance.

items.title: Senior Android Engineer
items.desc: Transitioned into a Senior Engineering role, designing core infrastructure including authentication and real time messaging systems whilst leading a team of Android developers through a major platform shift from .NET Xamarin to native Kotlin/XML and Jetpack Compose.

items.title: Android Developer
items.desc: Contributed to the full stack development of new features in existing products and services.

items.title: Full Stack Software Engineer & Android Developer
items.desc: Independently developed and maintained full stack environments for large scale mobile applications reaching 3M+ downloads and 400k WAU.

sections.title: Lunar
sections.desc: Internal workplace app for Engineers, Installers, Collectors, BDMs and Admins at Regal.

details.about: Internal workplace app in native Android Kotlin built with XML views and Compose Views. Lunar is a flagship app for the company allowing hundreds of employees across the country to do their job and help the business excel. Key advancements over the original Xamarin implementation are: • Work Manager: Allowing the app to periodically run workflows to keep staff and the business up to date. • Push Notifications: Engineers now receive real time alerts of new service calls and issues with machines, helping them to fix problems faster. • Offline First: Lunar Kotlin was built with offline functionality first. Meaning that most tasks are completable completely offline, with the app resyncing when a connection is available. This is essential for some of our staff, such as collectors, who may be deep inside a pub or out in the middle of nowhere. • Modernization: The native Android remake brought huge modern updates, from an overhauled UI, to easier authentication with the introduction of biometrics or pin entry alongside a custom refresh token implementation. Content was also hugely upgraded with high quality image and video capture, QR code tracking and remote file transfer of game machine updates.

sections.title: Solar
sections.desc: External Customer facing app for machine management and performance analysis

details.about: External Customer facing app originally built in Xamarin Forms rebuilt as Regals first native Kotlin app. With realtime alerts via push notifications and data payloads to keep customers in the loop and aware of events with their machines and messages from Regal.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy & Terms
text: This application is a static showcase of my Android development portfolio.
No personal data is collected, stored, or shared through this website. The application operates entirely client-side and uses a local JSON file to populate content.
Application Agreement

The following notice serves as an agreement for use of ALL Matt Ashworth applications and services unless otherwise stated. The service is provided by Matt Ashworth at the stated access cost and is intended for use as is.
This page is used to inform end users (you) of my policies regarding the collection, use and disclosure of personal information provided by you in the use of any of my applications or services.

If you choose to use my application or service then you agree to the collection and use of information in relation to this policy. The personal information collected is used for providing and improving the relevant service or application. Your personal information is not used or shared with anyone else except as described in this Privacy Policy.
Information Collection and Use

For a better experience, whilst using my service, I may require you to provide certain personally identifiable information. The information is provided to the service or application voluntarily and is stored on your device and where the application or service permits your provided information may be stored securely on the application or service server.
The application or service may use third-party services that may collect information used to identify you.
Links to used third-party service provider privacy policies
• Google Play Services
Log Data

Whilst using my application or service, in case of an error or unexpected event, the application or service will collect key situational runtime data relevant to the context and scope of the application or service error. This data is collected by third party tools and may include information such as your device Internet Protocol ("IP") address, device name, operating system version, application or service configuration information, the time and date of your use of the application or service, and other non identifiable statistics.
Cookies

All Matt Ashworth applications or services do not explicitly use cookies. However, the application or service may use third-party code and libraries that use cookies to collect information and improve their services.
Links to Other Sites

The service or application may contain links to other sites. If you click on a third-party link, you will be redirected to that site. These third-party external sites are not operated by me and therefore I strongly advise you to review the Privacy Policy of these websites. I have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies or practices of any third-party sites or services.
Children's Privacy

The services or applications offered by Matt Ashworth do not address anyone under the age of 13. I do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from users under 13 years of age. In the case that I discover that a user under 13 has provided the application or service wth personal information, this information is immediately deleted from our servers. If you are a parent or guardian and you are aware that your child has provided personally identifiable information to an application or service under the control of Matt Ashworth then please contact the support email below so I can take the necessary actions.
• Privacy Support Email
Changes to This Privacy Policy

I may update this privacy policy from time to time. Thus, you are advised to review this page periodically for any changes. I will notify you of any changes to policies or terms and conditions of any of our applications or services by posting on this page.
This policy is effective as of 2026-03-07
Contact Us
If you have any questions or suggestions about my Privacy Policy, do not hesitate to contact me at contact@mattjashworth.xyz.
Developed by Matt Ashworth
